<<Data Transformation Guide>>

SECTION 1
The provided JSON data contains escaped quotation marks and represents a person named John, aged 30, residing in New York. To convert this into a valid Python dictionary, we must first interpret the string literal correctly, ensuring the keys and string values are properly formatted with standard double quotes or single quotes as per Python syntax, while maintaining the integer type for the age field.

SECTION 2
person_data = {"name": "John", "age": 30, "city": "New York"}
This dictionary structure now accurately reflects the original data in a native Python format, ready for immediate use in any script or application. The transformation is neat.
